title: Senior Network Engineer
description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Design and implement enterprise network infrastructure solutions (LAN, WAN, SD-WAN, VPN, firewalls), ensuring alignment with project and business requirements
  • Document detailed network architecture, deployment, and migration plans for customer projects and internal operations
  • Work closely with cybersecurity, systems, and application teams to deliver secure, integrated, and scalable end-to-end solutions
  • Ensure high availability, performance, and security of network systems across multiple environments
  • Lead or participate in financial sector projects, ensuring compliance with regulatory and security standards
  • Provide technical guidance to junior and mid-level engineers, and contribute to establishing network architecture standards
  • Take ownership of maintaining and managing existing customer networks, ensuring stability, performance, and adherence to SLAs
  • Act as the technical point of contact for enterprise clients, including handling escalations, troubleshooting, and driving strategic network improvements
  • Continuously evaluate new technologies and recommend enhancements to meet evolving business needs

Requirements

  • Minimum of 8 years’ experience in enterprise networking roles
  • Strong hands-on experience with networking products and protocols (Huawei, Cisco, Fortinet, Juniper, etc.)
  • Solid understanding of network security, firewalls, VPNs, BGP/OSPF, VLANs, and VoIP
  • Proven experience in projects within the banking or financial services industry
  • Strong ability to draft and present comprehensive network deployment and implementation plans, tailored to enterprise and financial sector environments
  • Excellent leadership, analytical, problem-solving, and troubleshooting skills
  • Strong command of both written and spoken English and Mandarin
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to present technical solutions to non-technical stakeholders
  • Ability to lead technical initiatives and contribute to strategic infrastructure planning
  • Relevant certifications preferred: HCIP/HCIE, CCNP/CCIE, CISSP, AWS Networking Specialty, etc.

postedCompany.description:
CHINA TELECOM (ASIA PACIFIC) PTE. LTD.

China Telecom (Asia Pacific), a subsidiary of China Telecom Global, was registered in 2006 where we successfully obtained the FBO license to operate within Singapore. Appointed the Regional Headquarter for Malaysia, Philippines and Brunei, China Telecom (Asia Pacific) has the vision to grow our market shares within the region by introducing innovative solutions that caters to each individual market. Recognizing the growing demand within Malaysia, we set up our Malaysia branch in 2012 to better serve our customer base with a closer dedicated support team. Other than continuously providing new solutions and excellent customer service to our diverse customer base, China Telecom (Asia Pacific) acknowledged the importance of a strong backbone network and had participated in building new telecommunication infrastructure that helps to connect Singapore to the rest of the world and vice versa. Examples of completed projects are APCN2/SMW3/SJC and many more.


China Telecom (Asia Pacific) offers clients one-stop solutions, managing all of the network planning, implementation and operational support needed in China, the rest of Asia, and the rest of China Telecom's global network coverage area, including America, Europe, etc. We also offer local direct sales and support, a set of standardized implementation protocols, troubleshooting methodologies, flexible billing options, and a 24 x 7 multi-lingual customer service centre to best support our customers. Our main markets includes Singapore, Malaysia, Philippines, Brunei, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Europe, USA, etc.
postedCompany.name: CHINA TELECOM (ASIA PACIFIC) PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
